name: Demand Inelasticity
statement: Subscribers do not reduce consumption when quality degrades. They churn. Demand is inelastic to price within the loyalty window.
corollary: The intervention window — the period between first degradation and churn decision — is the operator's only actionable asset.
mechanism: Unlike most markets, telecom subscribers cannot easily reduce their network usage when quality drops. They need connectivity. When quality falls below tolerance, they do not consume less — they leave. The loyalty window varies by segment: enterprise contracts extend it, consumer prepaid compresses it to days. Whoever can identify the high-ARPU subscriber in that window can intervene.
atL4: At L4, the network identifies individual subscriber degradation in real time and triggers personalised intervention — proactive compensation, priority routing, or case management — before the subscriber is aware a problem occurred.
